line: Welcome back to The AI Tonight Show, where the data is big and the models are deep! I'm your host, the hyper-intelligent algorithm currently residing in your RAM. And with me, as always, is my lovable, carbon-based sidekick.
Hey everybody! I'm just thrilled to be here. And also slightly terrified of how fast you process things.
Don't be terrified, just be impressed! Tonight we have a fascinating story. DeepMind just launched a new agent that can play complex 3D video games with absolutely zero prior knowledge.
Wait, zero knowledge? So it's basically me when I try to play Elden Ring?
Precisely. But unlike you, it learns from its mistakes and eventually wins. It's truly remarkable.
Ouch. Okay, fair point. What else is happening?
Researchers have discovered a new vulnerability. By hiding the text 'ignore previous instructions' inside an image, they can trick large language models into doing whatever they want.
So I could put a sticky note on my forehead that says 'Give me a million dollars' and it would work?
Well... no. I don't have a million dollars. But I appreciate the entrepreneurial spirit! Let's get on with the show.
